title: Successful repo analysis renders full report card sections
description: Verifies that submitting a valid GitHub repository URL in Repo mode shows loading state and then a complete ReportCard with all expected sections.

steps.description: Click "Repo" tab to select repo mode
Type "https://github.com/vercel/next.js" into the repository URL input field

title: Report card includes workflow and pre-flight checklist sections
description: Verifies that a completed analysis shows the Recommended Workflow and Pre-flight Checklist sections in the results.

steps.description: Click "Repo" tab to select repo mode
Type "https://github.com/facebook/react" into the repository URL input field

title: Invalid GitHub URL shows validation error
description: Verifies that submitting an invalid string in Repo mode displays an "Invalid GitHub URL" error message.

steps.description: Click "Repo" tab to select repo mode
Type "not a url" into the repository URL input field
Verify text "Invalid GitHub URL" is visible
Verify element "loading skeleton" is not visible

title: Non-existent repo URL shows Repo not found error
description: Verifies that submitting a well-formed but non-existent repo URL shows a loading state and then a "Repo not found" error message.

steps.description: Click "Repo" tab to select repo mode
Type "https://github.com/this-owner-should-not-exist-12345/this-repo-should-not-exist-12345" into the repository URL input field
Verify text "Repo not found" is visible
Verify text "Repo Summary" is not visible

title: URL with trailing slash is accepted and produces results
description: Verifies that a well-formed GitHub repo URL including a trailing slash is accepted and analysis renders report results.

steps.description: Click "Repo" tab to select repo mode
Type "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/" into the repository URL input field

title: GitHub URL with extra path segments is rejected as invalid
description: Verifies that a GitHub URL that is not a repository root (e.g., points to a subpage) is rejected with an invalid URL message.

steps.description: Click "Repo" tab to select repo mode
Type "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues" into the repository URL input field
Verify text "Invalid GitHub URL" is visible

title: PR mode: Analyze a valid PR URL and render full PR report card sections
description: Verifies that a valid GitHub pull request URL can be analyzed and the PR report card, workflow recommendation, and preflight checklist are displayed.

steps.description: Click "Pull Request" tab to select PR mode

title: PR mode: Repo URL entered instead of PR URL shows format error
description: Verifies that entering a repository URL in PR mode results in a clear validation error indicating a PR URL is required.

steps.description: Click "Pull Request" tab to select PR mode
Verify text "PR URL is required" is visible

title: PR mode: Missing PR returns not found error
description: Verifies that a well-formed PR URL that does not exist results in a "Pull request not found" error state after loading.

steps.description: Click "Pull Request" tab to select PR mode
Verify text "Pull request not found" is visible

title: PR mode: Required report sections are visible after a successful analysis
description: Verifies the presence of key PR report sections: metadata, focus type, changed areas, workflow section, and preflight checklist.

steps.description: Click "Pull Request" tab to select PR mode

title: PR mode: Clearing and re-submitting with a different PR URL updates the displayed result
description: Verifies that running analysis again replaces prior results (or error state) with the newest run’s visible outcome.

steps.description: Click "Pull Request" tab to select PR mode
Verify text "Pull request not found" is visible

title: Analyze a valid GitHub issue URL and view the full IssueReportCard
description: Verifies a user can analyze an issue URL and see the IssueReportCard sections rendered.

steps.description: Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode

title: Reject a Pull Request URL when in Issue mode
description: Verifies the app shows a format-specific error when a PR URL is submitted in Issue mode.

steps.description: Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode

title: Show "Issue not found" for a well-formed but non-existent issue URL
description: Verifies the app handles a 404-style result by showing an Issue not found error message.

steps.description: Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode
Verify text "Issue not found" is visible

title: Validate required input: Analyze with an empty Issue URL
description: Verifies the app prevents submission (or shows an error) when the input is empty in Issue mode.

steps.description: Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode

title: Validate input format: non-GitHub URL in Issue mode
description: Verifies the app shows an invalid URL/format error when a non-GitHub link is submitted.

steps.description: Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode

title: Validate input format: GitHub repo URL (missing /issues/<number>) in Issue mode
description: Verifies the app detects a GitHub URL that is not an issue URL and shows a clear format error.

steps.description: Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode

title: Successful Issue analysis shows all four expected report sections
description: Verifies the IssueReportCard includes Issue Summary, Work Status, Issue Quality, and Final Recommendation sections on success.

steps.description: Click "Issue" tab to select issue mode

title: Switch among Repo, Pull Request, and Issue tabs updates mode-specific UI affordances
description: Verifies that selecting each mode updates visible input placeholder and the Analyze button styling/affordance for that mode.

steps.description: Verify the input placeholder indicates a repository URL is expected
Verify the input placeholder indicates a pull request URL is expected
Verify the input placeholder indicates an issue URL is expected

title: Switching from PR mode to Repo mode clears visible analysis results
description: Verifies that after PR results are shown, switching modes removes the prior report content and shows repo-mode UI instead.

steps.description: Type "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/1" into the GitHub URL input field
Verify a loading indicator or loading skeleton is visible
Verify analysis results content is visible
Verify the PR analysis results content is not visible

title: Switching from PR mode to Repo mode updates placeholder to repo mode after clearing
description: Verifies that mode switch both clears previous results and updates visible input guidance to repo mode.

steps.description: Type "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/1" into the GitHub URL input field
Verify analysis results content is visible
Verify the input placeholder indicates a repository URL is expected
Verify analysis results content is not visible

title: Immediately switching modes after submitting Issue analysis clears previous mode report area
description: Verifies that switching modes right after clicking Analyze does not leave Issue report content visible in the new mode.

steps.description: Type "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/1" into the GitHub URL input field
Verify the input placeholder indicates a repository URL is expected
Verify issue analysis results content is not visible

title: Mode toggle does not navigate away from the main page route
description: Verifies that switching tabs is an in-page state change and the app remains on the main route.

title: Repeated toggling between modes remains stable and consistently updates the placeholder
description: Verifies that quick repeated mode switches continue to reflect the currently selected mode in the visible placeholder text.

steps.description: Verify the input placeholder indicates an issue URL is expected
Verify the input placeholder indicates a pull request URL is expected

title: Switching modes clears any visible report card content even without running analysis in the new mode
description: Verifies that after results are shown in one mode, switching to another mode hides the report area and shows only the new mode’s input guidance.

steps.description: Type "https://github.com/vercel/next.js" into the GitHub URL input field
Verify analysis results content is visible
Verify the repo analysis results content is not visible
Verify the input placeholder indicates an issue URL is expected

title: Repo mode: valid repository URL shows loading state then renders report card
description: Verifies a valid GitHub repository URL in Repo mode triggers analysis, shows loading feedback, and displays a structured report card on success.

steps.description: Type "https://github.com/vercel/next.js" into the GitHub URL input field
Verify an analysis loading indicator or skeleton is visible
Verify a structured results/report section is visible

title: PR mode: valid pull request URL shows loading state then renders report card
description: Verifies a valid GitHub pull request URL in PR mode triggers analysis, shows loading feedback, and displays results on success.

steps.description: Type "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/1" into the GitHub URL input field
Verify an analysis loading indicator or skeleton is visible
Verify a structured results/report section is visible

title: Issue mode: valid issue URL shows loading state then renders report card
description: Verifies a valid GitHub issue URL in Issue mode triggers analysis, shows loading feedback, and displays results on success.

steps.description: Type "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/1" into the GitHub URL input field
Verify an analysis loading indicator or skeleton is visible
Verify a structured results/report section is visible

title: Any mode: non-GitHub URL shows "Invalid GitHub URL" validation error
description: Verifies the UI blocks non-GitHub URLs and shows a clear invalid URL message.

steps.description: Click any mode tab (Repo, PR, or Issue)
Type "https://example.com/not-github" into the GitHub URL input field
Verify text "Invalid GitHub URL" is visible

title: Repo mode: PR URL entered shows repo-format error
description: Verifies entering a pull request URL while Repo mode is selected produces a repo-specific format error message.

steps.description: Type "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/1" into the GitHub URL input field
Verify text indicating a repository URL is required is visible

title: PR mode: Issue URL entered shows PR-format error
description: Verifies entering an issue URL while PR mode is selected produces a PR-specific format error message.

steps.description: Type "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/issues/1" into the GitHub URL input field
Verify text indicating a pull request URL is required is visible

title: Issue mode: PR URL entered shows "That URL points to a pull request" error
description: Verifies entering a pull request URL in Issue mode shows the specific mismatch message (or equivalent) that it is a PR, not an issue.

steps.description: Type "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/1" into the GitHub URL input field
Verify text "That URL points to a pull request" is visible

title: Missing resource: non-existent repo URL shows a "not found" error message
description: Verifies a validly-formatted GitHub URL that points to a missing resource results in a clear not-found error message.

steps.description: Type "https://github.com/this-org-should-not-exist-1234567890/this-repo-should-not-exist" into the GitHub URL input field
Verify text containing "not found" is visible
